treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Zofran and Tylenol administered; Normal Saline 2L given.
treatment_information.treatment_response: On arrival to unit, patient stated pain resolved and was no longer nauseated.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient developed abdominal pain, vomiting, and diarrhea after eating a tuna sandwich.
Patient visited primary care physician and was sent to the ED.
In the ED, patient was afebrile and hemodynamically stable.
Labs revealed anion gap acidosis and mild transaminitis.
Patient given Zofran, Tylenol, Fosfomycin, and 2L of Normal Saline.
Patient reported resolution of pain and no longer felt nauseated.

patient_outcome.final_status: Discharged home in clear and coherent mental status.
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Patient advised to avoid contact with others for 72 hours due to norovirus.
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Patient diagnosed with gastroenteritis caused by Norovirus and advised on contagiousness.
